title: ProtocolLab WebTransport Performance Contracts
purpose: Define deterministic WebTransport-over-HTTP/3 bidirectional-stream and datagram workloads with exact session, payload, validation, metric, and artifact semantics.
scope: The family covers one authenticated WebTransport session with either one client-initiated bidirectional stream or 32 deterministic echoed WebTransport datagrams. It does not cover WebSocket, multiple streams, browser APIs, or MASQUE.

requirements.title: Deterministic WebTransport stream semantics
requirements.statement: The WebTransport bidirectional-stream scenario MUST declare the exact session count, stream count, endpoint, payload generator, payload size, payload hash, payload direction, and close code.

requirements.title: Exact WebTransport-over-HTTP/3 binding
requirements.statement: A WebTransport result MUST prove an HTTP/3 Extended CONNECT session with the webtransport protocol and reject HTTP request-response or WebSocket substitution.

requirements.title: WebTransport validity gate
requirements.statement: A passing WebTransport observation MUST validate session establishment, one client-initiated bidirectional stream, exact echoed byte count and SHA-256, clean close, zero unexpected failures, and zero timeouts.

requirements.title: WebTransport metrics and artifacts
requirements.statement: The WebTransport stream scenario MUST declare session rate, bidirectional byte rate, stream round-trip latency percentiles, operation outcomes, effective stream count, and raw validation, protocol proof, payload hash, stdout, stderr, and normalized result artifacts.

requirements.title: WebTransport comparison isolation
requirements.statement: WebTransport comparisons MUST match scenario version, protocol binding, data-path operation, endpoint semantics, payload identity, role, connection lifecycle, load profile, network profile, platform, topology, validation, and repetition policy.

requirements.title: Deterministic WebTransport datagram semantics
requirements.statement: The WebTransport datagram scenario MUST declare the exact session count, datagram count, bytes per datagram, endpoint, payload generator, payload-set hash, payload direction, and close code.

requirements.title: WebTransport datagram validity and evidence
requirements.statement: A passing WebTransport datagram observation MUST validate session establishment, negotiated datagram support, 32 sent and received datagrams, exact byte count and payload-set SHA-256, zero loss, clean close, zero unexpected failures, and zero timeouts, and MUST retain datagram rate, latency, byte, outcome, protocol-proof, raw-output, and normalized-result evidence.
